front: Concept: the suspension of judgment advocated by Pyrrhonian skeptics as the path to ataraxia; Edmund Husserl later used this Greek term for phenomenological 'bracketing'
Concept: Greek-language mental state usually translated as 'freedom from disturbance,' considered the highest good in both Pyrrhonian skepticism and Epicureanism
Philosopher: had a personal medal with the motto 'what do I know?' on one side and 'epoche' on the other; had skeptical quotes inscribed on the rafters of his tower study in Dordogne
Philosopher: Roderick Chisholm traced the 'Problem of the Criterion' through this thinker's version of a circular skeptical argument (diallelus)
Philosopher: extended skepticism to causation, arguing that when we observe 'constant conjunction,' we unjustly posit cause-and-effect out of habit
Concept: the idea that nothing can be known, advocated by Arcesilaus and other Academic skeptics
